facts.text: Ave Maria, Florida, was founded in 2005 by Ave Maria Development, a partnership of Barron Collier Companies and the Ave Maria Foundation led by Tom Monaghan, the Domino's Pizza founder.
The town’s centerpiece is the Ave Maria Oratory, a striking Catholic church that serves as a prominent landmark in the community.
Ave Maria University, opened on a temporary campus in 2003, anchors the town’s educational and cultural life.
In 2023, Ave Maria was the fastest‑growing community in the United States that was founded in the 21st century.
Collier County commissioners approved a 1,000‑acre expansion of Ave Maria in June 2023, bringing the town’s total size to 5,000 acres, the largest allowed under county rules.
The new land will mainly host single‑family homes, a new town center, and additional retail sites, expanding the community’s commercial offerings.
